Despite the inconsistency in the model of Star Destroyer used for the ''Tyrant'' in [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], the 2008 published {{StarshipsVehiclesCite|5|In Command}} identifies it as an ''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer.</ref>

The '''''Rebel Dream''''' was an [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]]<ref name="ISD" /> of the [[New Republic Defense Fleet/Legends|New Republic Navy]]. It began its career in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] as the '''''Tyrant'''''.

==History==
===Imperial service===
{{Quote|We're blind. We could be boarded by a Rebel ship at any minute.|Captain Lennox after the ''Tyrant'' was hit by an ion cannon during the Battle of Hoth|<ref name="TPOVDSC">{{SWGTCG|set=The Price of Victory|scenario=Dark Side scenario campaign}}</ref>}}
[[File:Lennox_Cabbel.jpg|thumb|left|250px|[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Xamuel Lennox|Lennox]] and [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Cabbel/Legends|Cabbel]] on the ''Tyrant'' at the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]]]
Assigned to [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] [[Death Squadron/Legends|Death Squadron]], the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] ''Tyrant'' was commanded by the capable [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Xamuel Lennox]], with [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Cabbel/Legends|Cabbel]] serving as [[Executive officer/Legends|executive officer]].<ref name="CCG-Cabbel" /><ref name="CCG-Lennox" /><ref name="In Command">{{StarshipsVehiclesCite|5|In Command}}</ref>

In [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], the ''Tyrant'' was one of the ten Imperial Star Destroyers involved in the [[Battle of Turkana/Legends|Battle of Turkana]].<ref name="IH:ACG" />

Eight months after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] [[Battle of the Poln system|fought]] [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Nuso Esva]]'s forces in the [[Poln system/Legends|Poln system]] as part of Death Squadron.<ref name="CoO" />

During the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], ''Tyrant'' attempted to intercept the first [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|transports]] to flee from [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], but was disabled by several shots from the Rebels' [[V-150 Planet Defender/Legends|planetary ion cannon]]. Lennox's warship was left to float through the [[Star system/Legends|system]], and several more Rebel transports subsequently escaped.<ref name="ESB" />

[[File:TyrantDisabled-BTCTG.png|thumb|left|250px|The [[Quantum Storm/Legends|''Quantum Storm'']] escaping the ''Tyrant'', disabled by the [[V-150 Planet Defender/Legends|planetary ion cannon]]]]
During the battle on the surface, Imperial troops captured Rebel [[General/Legends|General]] [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]] and sent him to the ''Tyrant''. After the shuttle entered the ''Tyrant''{{'s}} [[Hangar/Legends|docking bay]], the ''Tyrant'' was hit by another blast from the planetary ion cannon. While overseeing the repair of systems disabled by the ion cannon, Rebel [[Commando/Legends|commandos]] boarded the ship and rescued Madine. The commandos escaped with General Madine on a [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class shuttle]], with the ''Tyrant'' unable to pursue the shuttle. Later, the shuttle brought General Madine to the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Cruiser]] [[Champion (Mon Calamari cruiser)|''Champion'']], which then prepared to jump to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. Lennox engaged the cruiser with the now functional ''Tyrant'', and proceeded to launch [[TIE series/Legends|TIE fighters]] in preparation for battle. Lennox hailed the ''Champion'' and said that if they handed over General Madine, he would call off his attack. The captain of the ''Champion'' refused and ordered the launch of his ship's fighters. Captain Lennox was unable to defeat the ''Champion'', and the ship escaped with Madine.<ref name="TPOVDSC" /><ref name="TPOVLSC">{{SWGTCG|set=The Price of Victory|scenario=Light Side scenario campaign}}</ref>

In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Captain Lennox and the ''Tyrant'' participated in the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].<ref name="BtM" />

===New Republic service===
{{Quote|Then please, join me for dinner tonight, in my stateroom aboard the ''Rebel Dream''?|Minister of State Leia Organa Solo to Prince Isolder|<ref name="TCoPL" />}}
Later, in the aftermath of the [[Bacta War]], the New Republic captured the ''Tyrant'' from a weakly defended Imperial fueling outpost. Impressed by the symbolism of the Star Destroyer's former history serving with Vader's Death Squadron, Councilor [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] renamed the ship the ''Rebel Dream'' and selected it as her personal flagship.<ref name="TNEC" /><ref name="EC">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']]</ref>

By [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], the ''Rebel Dream'' was serving as [[Minister of State]] Leia Organa Solo's consular ship,<ref name="TCoPL" /> but was back in active duty to fight the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''[[Peremptory]]'' at the [[Battle of Storinal]]. In this battle, ''Rebel Dream'' was recaptured by Imperial forces.<ref name="TNEC" />

===Yuuzhan Vong War===
At the time of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], the New Republic had recovered the ''Rebel Dream''.<ref name="CSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref>

The ''Rebel Dream'' served under [[General/Legends|General]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] at [[Borleias/Legends|Borleias]]. The ''Rebel Dream''{{'s}} starfighters and support craft featured a white-and-dark-gray color scheme during the siege of Borleias. It later joined the fleet of the fledgling [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]], where it participated in the [[Defense of Mon Calamari (Yuuzhan Vong War)|defense]] of [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]].<ref name="TUF">''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|What a fantastic discovery! We had to look for ourselves to see that it was true. This is the first time anyone here has spotted this, Steve Sansweet included.|Leland Chee|<ref name="Insider">{{InsiderCite|98|Star Wars Q&A|''Star Wars'' Q&A}}</ref>}}
[[File:Tyrant-Insider98.jpg|thumb|right|315px|The picture of the ''Tyrant'' in the ''Star Wars Encyclopedia'' featured the inscription "We Break For Nobody," a nod to the movie ''Spaceballs'']]
The [[1998]] reference book ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', written by [[Stephen J. Sansweet]], contained a picture of the ''Tyrant'' which bore the inscription "We Break For Nobody" in [[High Galactic/Legends|High Galactic]] letters on its starboard side.<ref name="SWE">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> When asked about this in the [[Star Wars Insider 98|ninety-eighth issue]] of ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'', which was released in [[December]] [[2007]], [[Leland Chee]] declared that no one at [[Lucasfilm]] had spotted it before, Sansweet included.<ref name="Insider" /> He explained that many of the images used in the ''Star Wars Encyclopedia'' were reused from [[Decipher]]'s [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]],<ref name="Insider" /> though the words did not appear on the ''Tyrant'' card.<ref name="CCG-Tyrant">{{CCG|set=Hoth Limited|cardname=''Tyrant''}}</ref> Chee hypothesized that the inscription was added by someone at Decipher before the art was delivered to Lucasfilm, or that it was added by an art editor working on the reference book. He also stated that it was clearly a nod to the [[1987]] parody film [[Wikipedia:Spaceballs|''Spaceballs'']], though the word "brake" was changed to "break," not knowing whether the spelling was intentional, or some sort of comment on working conditions. Chee assured that the inscription was not on the [[Industrial Light & Magic|ILM]] model, as none of the Star Destroyer props from the [[original trilogy]] had any visible markings, and that it had not appeared in any other source.<ref name="Insider" />
